critical_findings.title: AG-01 continuation is not strictly enforcement-authoritative
critical_findings.description: run_agent_golden_path derives continuation from decision.system_response and can diverge from enforcement.final_status in review-required paths. [id=AG-01; severity=P0; file=spectrum_systems/modules/runtime/agent_golden_path.py; function=run_agent_golden_path; minimal_fix=FIX-001]
critical_findings.why_dangerous: This creates a split-brain seam where final execution can be reported as success while enforcement indicates require_review. [impact=execution status can contradict enforcement authority]
critical_findings.failure_scenario: A control decision resolves to warn/require_review; enforcement returns require_review but final execution record remains success due to decision-based continuation logic. [failure_mode=decision-based continuation bypasses enforcement-final-status authority]

required_fixes.description: Make AG-01 continuation/status derivation strictly enforcement.final_status-based and keep execution record fail-closed when enforcement is not allow.
Constrain evaluation_control runtime path to replay_result-only inputs (or fail closed when eval_summary mode is used in runtime seams).
Add monitor replay-boundary lineage assertion: error_budget_status.observability_metrics_id must reference embedded observability_metrics.artifact_id.
Validate and bind control_loop.trace_context to replay artifact identity to eliminate replay/control context split-brain paths.

optional_improvements: Keep replay-related helper builders aligned with replay-authoritative lineage assumptions to avoid normalizing weaker fixture patterns.
Retain isolated trace_store injection discipline in chaos cases to avoid accidental fallback to process-global trace state.
failure_mode_summary: Replay-authoritative enforcement is significantly improved, but one critical enforcement-vs-execution split-brain defect and additional replay-boundary consistency gaps remain open.
